answer:
**In *Diwan-e-Ghalib*, 'manzil' (destination/halting-place) appears with both literal and metaphorical senses, as in these couplets (sher).**[^1][^2]

**Literal halting-place (physical journey):**  
> With every step, the goal recedes in vain from me;  
> My very speed makes the whole desert plain flee from me.[^1]

Here, *manzil* is the concrete stopping-point or endpoint of a desert trek, evoked by caravan-travel imagery: *qadam* (steps), *raftaar* (pace/speed), *biyaban* (wilderness/desert), and *doori* (distance). The mirage-like retreat of the goal amid motion underscores futile wandering.[^3]

**Sufi spiritual stage:**  
> My heart’s a lane for thoughts of wine and revelry,  
> But my soul on the path to piety—it was not to be.[^2]

*Manzil* denotes a mystical station (*maqam*) on the Sufi path (*suluk*), specifically *sar-manzil-e-taqwa* (pinnacle stage of piety/God-fearing devotion). The contrast with worldly indulgence (*khayal-e-mai-o-saaghar*, thoughts of wine/cups) and *jaada-e*/*nafas* (soul's advance/breath as spiritual progression) shifts it to esoteric ascent, unattained.[^4]

**Polysemy activation:** Primarily **context** and **company of nearby words**. Literal use clusters with physical travel lexicon (*qadam*, *raftaar*, *biyaban*); spiritual pairs with Sufi-journey terms (*jaada*, *sar-manzil*, *taqwa*). Syntax reinforces: genitive *doori-e-manzil* (distance-*of*-goal, spatial) vs. compound *sar-manzil-e-taqwa* (head-goal-*of*-piety, hierarchical stage). Ghazal radif/refrain (*...na hua*) amplifies ambiguity playfully.[^5]

[^1]: Mirza Ghalib, *Diwan-E-Ghalib*, ch. 67: "Har qadam doori-e-manzil hai numayan mujh se" [paragraph](/works/mirza-ghalib-diwan-e-ghalib-74ed4c/67-har-qadam-doori-e-manzil-hai-numayan-mujh-se/translation/#p-08026e).

[^2]: Mirza Ghalib, *Diwan-E-Ghalib*, ch. 29: "Dehr mein naqsh-e-wafa wajah-e-tasalli na hua" [paragraph](/works/mirza-ghalib-diwan-e-ghalib-74ed4c/29-dehr-mein-naqsh-e-wafa-wajah-e-tasalli-na-hua/translation/#p-726f9a).

[^3]: Full ch. 67 context sustains travel motif (desert fire, blisters, shadow fleeing). [chapter](/works/mirza-ghalib-diwan-e-ghalib-74ed4c/67-har-qadam-doori-e-manzil-hai-numayan-mujh-se/translation/).

[^4]: Taqwa as Sufi *maqam* (stage); cf. classical *manazil al-sa'irin*. Full ch. 29 contrasts faith's failure with revelry. [chapter](/works/mirza-ghalib-diwan-e-ghalib-74ed4c/29-dehr-mein-naqsh-e-wafa-wajah-e-tasalli-na-hua/translation/).

[^5]: Work-wide wiki notes travel collocations in Ghalib; polysemy via radif common in Urdu ghazal. [wiki card](/works/mirza-ghalib-diwan-e-ghalib-74ed4c/wiki/67-har-qadam-doori-e-manzil-hai-numayan-mujh-se.card.md).
